title: Sources of non-determinism in training
description: Results vary across runs from RNG seeds, data shuffling, non-deterministic GPU kernels, and library/hardware versions.
body: Use when two runs of the same code give different metrics. Non-determinism comes from unseeded RNGs (init, dropout, shuffling), parallel reductions and atomics in GPU kernels, varying library/driver versions, and hardware differences. Do not expect bit-identical results across machines even with seeds. Actions: seed all RNGs, enable deterministic kernels (accepting a speed cost), pin library versions, and record the environment with each run. Validation signals include a reproduced metric under fixed seed and pinned versions. Risk boundary: claiming an improvement without controlling these sources can report noise as signal; require reproduction before trusting a result.
